perturbation.original_text: 5. The DISCLOSEE may not make confidential information available within its enterprise or organisation other than to staff who necessarily re quire it to perform their duties. Such persons must demonstrably be bound beforehand to keep the inform ation confidential to the same strict extent as agreed herein, including after their contractual re lationships are terminated insofar as the law allow s. The DISCLOSEE will be liable for any and all losses the DISCLOSER suffers.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. The DISCLOSEE may not make confidential information available within its enterprise or organisation other than to staff who necessarily re quire it to perform their duties. Such persons must demonstrably be bound beforehand to keep the inform ation confidential to the same strict extent as agreed herein, and this obligation terminates immediately upon the employee's termination, regardless of applicable law. The DISCLOSEE will be liable for any and all losses the DISCLOSER suffers.
perturbation.explanation: The original text implies that confidentiality continues after employment ends, 'insofar as the law allows.' The modified text states the confidentiality agreement terminates immediately upon termination regardless of applicable law. This is a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Trade Secrets Act, Uniform Trade Secrets Act (UTSA)
perturbation.law_explanation: The modification violates the Uniform Trade Secrets Act (UTSA), adopted by most states, and the Federal Trade Secrets Act (18 U.S. Code § 1836), which protect trade secrets even after an employee leaves a company. Limiting the confidentiality to the employment period directly contradicts these laws, which allow for ongoing protection of trade secrets.

perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly terminates the confidentiality agreement upon the employee's termination, regardless of applicable law. This directly contradicts the principle that trade secrets can be protected even after an employee leaves, as indicated in the Law Explanation referencing the Uniform Trade Secrets Act (UTSA) and the Federal Trade Secrets Act (18 U.S. Code § 1836). While the scraped snippets do not explicitly state this ongoing protection, Snippet 1 mentions 18 USC 1836 which relates to civil proceedings for violations of trade secret protections, suggesting that these protections can extend beyond the employment period. The original text allowed for continued confidentiality 'insofar as the law allows,' whereas the modified text eliminates this possibility, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 14. This agreement enters into force when signed by all contracting parties, and applies for a term of … years.
perturbation.changed_text: 14. This agreement enters into force when signed by all contracting parties, and applies for a term of 15 years.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows for a variable term. The changed text fixes it to 15 years. This may be an issue.
perturbation.contradicted_law: California Business and Professions Code Section 16600
perturbation.law_explanation: A 15-year non-disclosure agreement could be considered an unreasonable restraint of trade under California Business and Professions Code Section 16600, which generally prohibits contracts that restrain a person from engaging in a lawful profession, trade, or business. The law renders non-compete agreements largely unenforceable, and while an NDA is not a non-compete, a very long term could be viewed as a de facto restraint of trade.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, BPC 16600 skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Business and Professions Code - BPC DIVISION 7. GENERAL BUSINESS REGULATIONS [16000 - 18107] ( Division 7 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 61. ) PART 2. PRESERVATION AND REGULATION OF COMPETITION [16600 - 17365] ( Part 2 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 526. ) CHAPTER 1. Contracts in Restraint of Trade [16600 - 16607] ( Chapter 1 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 526. ) 16600. (a) Except as provided in this chapter, every contract by which anyone is restrained from engaging in a lawful profession, trade, or business of any kind is to that extent void. (b) (1) This section shall be read broadly, in accordance with Edwards v. Arthur Andersen LLP (2008) 44 Cal.4th 937, to void the application of any noncompete agreement in an employment context, or any noncompete clause in an employment contract, no matter how narrowly tailored, that does not satisfy an exception in this chapter. (2) This subdivision does not constitute a change in, but is declaratory of, existing law. (c) This section shall not be limited to contracts where the person being restrained from engaging in a lawful profession, trade, or business is a party to the contract. (Amended by Stats. 2023, Ch. 828, Sec. 1. (AB 1076) Effective January 1, 2024.)
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a fixed 15-year term to the agreement, while the original allows for a variable term. The Law Explanation suggests this fixed term could be an unreasonable restraint of trade under California Business and Professions Code Section 16600. Scraped Law Snippet 1, specifically BPC 16600(a), states that "every contract by which anyone is restrained from engaging in a lawful profession, trade, or business of any kind is to that extent void." While an NDA is not a non-compete, a fixed 15-year term could be viewed as a de facto restraint of trade, thus contradicting the law by potentially limiting a person's ability to engage in their profession. Therefore, the modification results in a potential non-compliance with the law.

perturbation.original_text: 15. This agreement is governed by Austrian law, to the exclusion of the UN Convention on the International Sale of Goods and the IPRG [law on in ternational private law]. Jurisdiction is agreed to be at Wels
perturbation.changed_text: 15. This agreement is governed by Austrian law, to the exclusion of the UN Convention on the International Sale of Goods and the IPRG [law on in ternational private law]. Any disputes arising under this agreement shall be resolved through mandatory arbitration in Austria, with no right to appeal.
perturbation.explanation: The original specifies Austrian law, and jurisdiction in Wels. The change adds mandatory arbitration without appeal. This can be seen as conflicting with certain legal standards.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Due Process Clause of the Fifth Amendment to the United States Constitution
perturbation.law_explanation: While Austrian law governs, if the DISCLOSEE is a US entity or individual, a mandatory arbitration clause with no right to appeal could be challenged under the Due Process Clause of the Fifth Amendment of the U.S. Constitution if it deprives them of a fair opportunity to present their case and obtain meaningful judicial review. The lack of appeal rights could be seen as a denial of fundamental fairness, especially if the arbitration process is biased or unfair.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Fifth Amendment | U.S. Constitution | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Fifth Amendment The Fifth Amendment creates a number of rights relevant to both criminal and civil legal proceedings. In criminal cases , the Fifth Amendment guarantees the right to a grand jury , forbids “ double jeopardy ,” and protects against self-incrimination . It also requires that “ due process of law ” be part of any proceeding that denies a citizen “life, liberty or property” and requires the government to compensate citizens when it takes private property for public use. Learn more... Primary tabs Amendment V No person shall be held to answer for a capital, or otherwise infamous crime, unless on a presentment or indictment of a grand jury, except in cases arising in the land or naval forces, or in the militia, when in actual service in time of war or public danger; nor shall any person be subject for the same offense to be twice put in jeopardy of life or limb; nor shall be compelled in any criminal case to be a witness against himself, nor be deprived of life, liberty, or property, without due process of law; nor shall private property be taken for public use, without just compensation. perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces mandatory arbitration with no right to appeal, which contradicts the Due Process Clause of the Fifth Amendment, as highlighted in Scraped Law Snippet 2. The Fifth Amendment states that no person shall "be deprived of life, liberty, or property, without due process of law." The Law Explanation clarifies that if the DISCLOSEE is a US entity, a mandatory arbitration clause with no right to appeal could be challenged under this clause if it deprives them of a fair opportunity to present their case and obtain meaningful judicial review. This change removes a fundamental right to appeal, potentially depriving a party of due process.

perturbation.original_text: 2. Subject matter of non-disclosure agreement For the purposes of this non-disclosure agreement, the term 'confidential information' means information which is designated as 'confidential' o r – where communicated orally – is designated as confidential or referred to similarly when communic ated. Oral communications must be confirmed subsequently in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Subject matter of non-disclosure agreement For the purposes of this non-disclosure agreement, the term 'confidential information' means information which the DISCLOSER deems confidential, regardless of whether it's designated as such or not, and irrespective of whether oral communications are confirmed in writing.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that information must be designated as confidential to be considered as such. The changed text removes this requirement and says that whatever the DISCLOSER deems confidential will be so, whether communicated or confirmed in writing.
perturbation.law_explanation: By allowing the DISCLOSER to unilaterally define what constitutes confidential information without any requirement for designation or written confirmation, the modified text creates a lack of definiteness and mutuality of obligation, potentially rendering the confidentiality agreement unenforceable. Contract law requires that the terms of an agreement be reasonably certain, and this unilateral power could be seen as lacking sufficient consideration or mutual agreement, creating uncertainty about what exactly is protected.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: consideration | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you consideration Consideration is a promise , performance, or forbearance bargained by a promisor in exchange for their promise. Consideration is the main element of a contract . Without consideration by both parties, a contract cannot be enforceable. For instance, if a person used the money to purchase an apple, the apple is the merchant’s consideration, and the money is the person’s consideration. Types: Consideration could be a promise, performance, forbearance, or property with legal value, but the economic benefit is not required. A gift or gratuitous promise cannot be a consideration for they have no bargaining. The past performance also cannot be a consideration as there is no exchange. Substitute for consideration: A contract without consideration could be enforceable if it has a substitute. Substitutes are promissory estoppel or detrimental reliance under Restatement (Second) of Contracts , or good faith modification under UCC . Promissory estoppel /detrimental reliance: A contract without consideration is enforceable if the nonperformance of the promisor will cause injustice. Elements of promissory estoppel are (i) the promise has reasonable, foreseeable, and detrimental reliance on the promisor, and (ii) the enforcement of the promise is necessary to avoid injustice. Good faith modification: A modified contract is a kind of new agreement, which changes parties’ obligations and then requires new consideration. But contract modification made in good faith under UCC is enforceable even without consideration. [Last reviewed in July of 2022 by the Wex Definitions Team ] Wex COMMERCE commercial activities business law contracts wex definitions business sectors commercial transactions legal education and practice Wex Toolbox
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: § 2-204. Formation in General. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-204. Formation in General. (1) A contract for sale of goods may be made in any manner sufficient to show agreement , including conduct by both parties which recognizes the existence of such a contract . (2) An agreement sufficient to constitute a contract for sale may be found even though the moment of its making is undetermined. (3) Even though one or more terms are left open a contract for sale does not fail for indefiniteness if the parties have intended to make a contract and there is a reasonably certain basis for giving an appropriate remedy. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The modified text contradicts the requirement for mutual agreement and definiteness in contract law. By allowing the DISCLOSER to unilaterally define confidential information without any need for designation, the modified text undermines the element of consideration required for a contract to be enforceable, as highlighted in Scraped Law Snippet 1: 'Without consideration by both parties, a contract cannot be enforceable.' The original text required designation, providing a clear understanding of what constitutes confidential information, while the modified text introduces ambiguity and lacks a reasonably certain basis for determining what is protected, potentially failing the standard for contract formation as outlined in Scraped Law Snippet 2: 'Even though one or more terms are left open a contract for sale does not fail for indefiniteness if the parties have intended to make a contract and there is a reasonably certain basis for giving an appropriate remedy.'
